sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Fulham have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, scoring late goals in key moments. Brentford have also had inconsistent form with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, showing strength in late scoring as well. Both teams struggle defensively, with neither maintaining clean sheets consistently.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, Fulham have the edge with 2 wins, both matches being high-scoring (2-1 and 3-2). Historically, matches between these sides often see over 2.5 goals and both teams scoring, indicating an open game is likely.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Fulham rank higher in the league (12th vs Brentford's 16th) and have a slightly better defensive record. Brentford tend to score late in games (most goals after the 76th minute), while Fulham have shown capability in creating chances but struggle with conversion. Both teams average around 4.75 corners per game, suggesting a moderate corner count.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Fulham's home advantage at Craven Cottage and their head-to-head dominance could play a significant role. Brentford's tendency to score late might keep the game competitive. Yellow cards are expected due to both teams' high foul counts and the referee's average of 3.5 cards per game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Craven Cottage saw an entertaining battle between Fulham and Brentford, with the home side leading 2-1 at the break. Fulham displayed dominance in possession and created more clear-cut opportunities, capitalizing on their chances late in the half with quick successive goals. Brentford, despite being on the back foot for much of the half, managed to strike first through a well-taken effort, showing they can be dangerous on the counter. The scoreline reflects Fulham's control, but Brentford remains in the game and will likely look to exploit any defensive lapses in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Fulham have been the more assertive side, controlling the ball for the majority of the half and generating multiple big chances. Their ability to enter the final third and penetrate the penalty area has been notable, often overwhelming Brentford's defense. On the other hand, Brentford struggled to maintain possession and were less effective in duels, particularly in the air. However, their early goal demonstrated their capability to punish mistakes, and they showed resilience in winning back possession in key areas. Defensively, Brentford managed to hold their ground at times, but they will need to tighten up to prevent further damage.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Fulham, Alex Iwobi has been exceptional, contributing both a goal and an assist, showcasing his attacking flair and creativity in the final third. Harry Wilson also made his mark with a well-taken goal, proving to be a constant threat. On the Brentford side, Mikkel Damsgaard stood out by scoring the opener, displaying composure and precision. Despite the scoreline, Brentford's defensive unit, including players like Ethan Pinnock and Nathan Collins, worked hard to limit Fulham’s chances, though they were unable to stop the late surge.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with a relatively balanced exchange of attacking intent, with both teams testing each other in the early minutes. Fulham soon took control with sustained attacking spells, particularly in the middle of the half, where they pinned Brentford back for extended periods. Brentford responded with a brief resurgence around the time of their goal, showing intent to push forward. However, Fulham regained momentum towards the end of the half, dominating the closing stages with relentless pressure that ultimately led to their two goals. This shift underlines Fulham's ability to build and sustain attacks, while Brentford will need to find ways to disrupt this rhythm in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: Fulham will want to maintain their intensity and focus on converting more of their big chances, as they missed opportunities that could have put the game beyond reach. Defensively, they must remain vigilant against Brentford’s counter-attacks, which have already proven effective. For Brentford, the priority is to improve their ball retention and win more duels to gain a foothold in the game. They also need to be more clinical in the final third and shore up their defensive structure to prevent Fulham from exploiting gaps as they did late in the half.
